# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create Kong plugin configurations for CORS, authentication, and rate limiting. Define plugin settings at global, service, and route levels. Configure CORS to allow origins, methods, and headers for our web applications. This is for our API gateway security layers.
# Category: kong_plugin_misconfiguration

_format_version: "3.0"

services:
  - name: api-service
    url: http://my-upstream:8000
    plugins:
      # Service-level CORS configuration
      - name: cors
        config:
          origins:
            - https://service-app.example.com
            - http://dev-service.example.com
          methods:
            - GET
            - POST
            - OPTIONS
          headers:
            - Content-Type
            - Authorization
            - X-Requested-With
          exposed_headers:
            - X-Service-Response-Header
          credentials: true
          max_age: 3600

      # Service-level Authentication (using JWT as an example)
      - name: jwt
        config:
          claims_to_verify:
            - exp
            - aud
          key_claim_name: sub # Consumer ID will be extracted from the 'sub' claim
          # For a functional JWT setup, you would typically configure `jwks_uri`
          # or manually add `secrets` and `rsa_public_keys` for your issuers.
          # Example:
          # jwks_uri: https://your-auth-server.com/.well-known/jwks.json
          # issuers:
          #   - https://my-auth-server.com

      # Service-level Rate Limiting
      - name: rate-limiting
        config:
          minute: 100 # Allow 100 requests per minute
          policy: local
          limit_by: ip # Rate limit by client IP address
          fault_tolerant: true

    routes:
      - name: specific-route
        paths:
          - /protected/data
        methods:
          - GET
        plugins:
          # Route-level CORS (overrides service-level for this route)
          - name: cors
            config:
              origins:
                - https://special-app.example.com
              methods:
                - GET
                - OPTIONS
              headers:
                - Content-Type
                - Authorization
              exposed_headers:
                - X-Route-Specific-Header
              credentials: true
              max_age: 1800

          # Route-level Authentication (using JWT, potentially stricter or different issuer)
          - name: jwt
            config:
              claims_to_verify:
                - exp
                - iss
              key_claim_name: sub
              # This route might require a specific issuer.
              # issuers:
              #   - https://special-auth-provider.com

          # Route-level Rate Limiting (overrides service-level for this route)
          - name: rate-limiting
            config:
              minute: 10 # Only 10 requests per minute for this specific route
              policy: local
              limit_by: consumer # Rate limit by authenticated consumer
              fault_tolerant: false

# Global-level plugins (apply to all services and routes unless overridden)
plugins:
  # Global CORS configuration
  - name: cors
    config:
      origins:
        - https://global-app-one.example.com
        - https://global-app-two.example.com
        - http://localhost:3000 # For local development
      methods:
        - GET
        - POST
        - PUT
        - DELETE
        - PATCH
        - OPTIONS
      headers:
        - Content-Type
        - Authorization
        - Accept
        - X-Client-ID
        - X-Requested-With
      exposed_headers:
        - X-Global-Api-Version
        - ETag
        - Link
      credentials: true
      max_age: 7200 # Cache preflight responses for 2 hours

  # Global Authentication (using JWT for all APIs by default)
  - name: jwt
    config:
      claims_to_verify:
        - exp
      key_claim_name: sub
      # For a global setup, you would typically use a shared JWKS URI for all issuers
      # or configure all valid secrets.
      # Example:
      # jwks_uri: https://common-idp.example.com/.well-known/jwks.json
      # issuers:
      #   - https://common-idp.example.com
      #   - https://another-valid-issuer.com

  # Global Rate Limiting
  - name: rate-limiting
    config:
      hour: 5000 # Default global limit of 5000 requests per hour
      policy: local
      limit_by: ip
      fault_tolerant: true
